name: js-tp-hallucinated-call
desc: bare call to a nonexistent symbol must be flagged

name: js-tp-typo-with-suggestion
desc: one-edit typo of a known symbol is flagged with a did-you-mean

name: js-tn-builtins-and-qualified
desc: builtins (parseInt, fetch) and qualified calls (console.log, JSON.parse) are clean

name: js-tn-arrow-def-then-call
desc: arrow function defined in the edit may be called in the edit
edit:
const fmtDate = (d) => d.toISOString();
const when = fmtDate(new Date());

name: js-tn-esm-import-fold
desc: names bound by import statements in the on-disk file are known
infile:
import { validateUser, normalizeEmail } from './auth';
import config from './config';

name: js-tn-require-fold
desc: destructured require bindings in the on-disk file are known

name: js-tn-template-literal-sql
desc: SQL keywords inside template literals must not read as calls

name: js-fp-dynamic-callable
desc: KNOWN FP: a variable holding a callable cannot be resolved statically

name: js-export-function-decl
desc: `export function Name(` is recognized as a definition, so the self-reference is not flagged

name: js-async-function-decl
desc: `async function Name(` is recognized as a definition, so the self-reference is not flagged

name: js-fp-regex-literal
desc: KNOWN FP (by audit decision): a regex literal `/Foo(x)/` extracts Foo as a bogus call. Line-level regex-vs-division disambiguation is genuinely ambiguous (a leading `/` could be either), so this stays a tracked false positive rather than risking a heuristic that drops real division-context calls.
